title: Pseudopanax laetevirens
taxonomy: Pseudopanax laetevirens is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Araliaceae. It belongs to the genus Pseudopanax, which is a group of flowering plants native to New Zealand. The species Pseudopanax laetevirens is a small, deciduous shrub that is native to the North Island of New Zealand. It is often used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive foliage and compact growth habit. Pseudopanax laetevirens is also known as the shining broadleaf or shining woodland flax.
uses: Pseudopanax laetevirens, also known as the Red Lustre or Yellow Maple, has limited information available on its medicinal uses.
